summarization.a: The image displays a contours of the pressure of HF (Hydrofluoric acid) over a wafer across four different conditions  for reactor G0. The color gradient represents the pressure values, with red indicating higher pressures and blue indicating lower pressures.
summarization.b: The image displays a contours of the pressure of HF (Hydrofluoric acid) over a wafer across four different conditions  for reactor G1.
summarization.c: The image displays a contours of the pressure of HF (Hydrofluoric acid) over a wafer across four different conditions  for reactor G2.
summarization.d: The image displays a contours of the pressure of HF (Hydrofluoric acid) over a wafer across four different conditions  for reactor G3.

c.question: Why G1 has less deviation than G0 and G2?
c.answer: The flow of the reactor with three inlets (G1) is more evenly distributed than that of G0, and consequently, G1 has less pressure deviation than G0.

d.question: Does the  images represent CFD simulation?

b.question: What is the process condition?
b.answer: A process time of 0.1 s and a HF feed flow rate of 150 sccm.
